title: antennaria howellii subsp. gaspensis
intro: Antennaria howellii subsp. gaspensis, also known as the Gaspé yarrow, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the sunflower family, characterized by its small white or pink flowers. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it effective in treating wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Gaspé yarrow include reducing inflammation, relieving stress and anxiety, and promoting overall well-being. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, making it a valuable addition to traditional medicine.
